creatures.name: Dracoviper
creatures.upkeep: Metaturnal; Active during both the day and the night, sleeping sporadically Preening; gently nip a teammate to pluck away loose scales. Rubbing; rub throughout both. against sharp rocks or trees to clean off parasites. Use Isleep after 5 minutes of rest during preferred time. Use Iclean after.
creatures.profileHtml:
Dracoviper

Tier: 3 Group Size: 4 Gondwa Habitat: Red Island, Ripple Beach, Golden Plateau, Ocean Stacks, White Cliffs, Sharptooth Marsh, Castaway Isle, The Mudflats, Rainbow Hills, Sand Caverns. Riparia Habitat: Wind Tunnels, Hollow Hills, Wollemi Forest, Dry Fang Canyon, Cliff Edge Falls, Crag Bluffs, East Passage, Palm Islands.
Nesting must always happen in a habitat.
Gondwa Courting zone: In autumn, Dracos meet on Red Island to court. Riparia Courting Zone: In autumn, Dracos meet in Wind Tunnels to court.
!sleep, !tasty and !clean can all be used upon entering the courting zone in the required season. If nested into a courting zone in the correct season, you may use all comfort commands once adol.
Preferred Foods: Dracoviper, Griffin, Dominus, Arowana (Critter), Perch (Critter), Kelp Shark (Critter).
Use !tasty in local chat when eating preferred food.
Active Time: Metaturnal; Active during both the day and the night, sleeping sporadically throughout both.
Use !sleep after 5 minutes of rest during preferred time.
Upkeep: Preening; gently nip a teammate to pluck away loose scales. Rubbing; rub against sharp rocks or trees to clean off parasites.
Use !clean after.

Behaviors

Festival Of Kites
In autumn, unmated Dracovipers migrate to Red Island or Wind Tunnels. There, they fly, dive, and call out to show their strength and skill. The fastest and most agile flyers stand out. After landing, brightly colored Dracovipers may approach duller ones and spread their wings to ask for a partnership. If accepted, both rumble and stretch their wings. If rejected, the response may be biting, spitting, and angry roaring. Less colorful Dracovipers often land and watch instead of approaching others. A Dracoviper does not have to join a swarm, but refusing may force it to live alone for several months. Solo Dracovipers struggle and cannot use !sleep unless they offer food to a swarm in hopes of joining. When challenged by a swarm, a solo Dracoviper may make a friendly call to show it wants to join.
Crowded Airspace
Due to their wide wingspan, Dracovipers are limited to a defense/offense limit of 3. They rely on their barrel-roll for aerial tactics, and having any more Dracos in the air results in accidental friendly-fire. During life or death fights against T5-6, 4 Dracovipers may participate in combat/defense.
Give me SPACE
Dracoviper will only warn once (aggressive call) with no wait before wing slamming to shove creatures too close away from them. (DO NOT shove people off cliffs.)
Defense
Dracovipers may attack Griffins they see on Red Island at 75% hunger. Dracoviper may also bully Griffins off of a BD (through normal BD contesting rules) at 70% hunger.

Basic Info

What may be considered a rulebreak?
May attack griffins on red island at 75% hunger May contest for a Griffins BD at 70% hunger
Can this creature symbiote? To what tier?
No
Can this creature have a symbiote?
Yes

creatures.name: Sarcosuchus
creatures.upkeep: Nocturnal; Active in the night, sleeps during the day. Mud Bathing, Preening Use Isleep after 5 minutes of rest during preferred time. Use Iclean after.
creatures.profileHtml:
Sarcosuchus

Tier: 4 Group Size: 3 Habitat: Young Grove, Titan's Pass, Dark Woods, Birchwoods, Whistling Columns, Snake Gully, Hunter's Thicket, Triad Falls, Big Quill Lake, Hot Springs
Nesting must always happen in a habitat.
Courting Zone
In Winter, Sarcosuchus meet in Sharptooth Marsh to court.
Preferred Foods: Lurdusaurus, Deer, Wolves, Cougar, Utahraptor, Mawsonia (Critter)
Use !tasty in local chat when eating preferred food.
Active Time: Nocturnal; Active in the night, sleeps during the day.
Use !sleep after 5 minutes of rest during preferred time.
Upkeep: Mud Bathing, Preening
Use !clean after.

Behaviors

Ambush
Sarcosuchus are weaker than their kin and they know it, picking and choosing their battles with careful planning and precision. This crocodile prefers not to hunt creatures that can see it in the water, only hunting where it is deep enough to hide them completely. If they are staging an ambush and get within one of their own length of the prey, and are spotted, they may continue to engage. If a perfect unseen ambush has been executed they may use !tasty when eating.
Warm Season Surge
Sarcosuchus does not have to territory each other during the Summer, the perfect season for sunbathing. They will often sit on the shores and rocks of lakes and rivers, opening their mouth as they regulate their internal temperatures. Sarcosuchus lazily hangs around with its gaping maw, the ideal opportunity to let small creatures eat the shreds of meat caught between its teeth. This dynamo of a crocodile will contest others of its kind for the best sunbathing spots. Adult crocs will always take claim to a spot before Sub-Adults and below. Initiate a spar to vie for a spot, ending when one member submits or reaches below 50% health.
Mud Slinking
Sarcosuchus may wear mud and stalk around water sources ambushing these they believe they can pick up. If able to grab their prey, they quickly dash to the nearest water to drown it. If they attempt to grab something and they fail, they flee to water out of fear of retaliation.

Basic Info

What may be considered a rulebreak.
May use !tasty if Sarcosuchus was completely hidden and successfully ambushed its prey.
Can this creature symbiote? To what tier?
No
Can this creature have a symbiote? How many? What tier?
Yes, 2 T1, 1 T2

creatures.name: Deinonychus
creatures.upkeep: Diurnal; Active in the day, sleeps during the night. Preening/Dust-bathing; shake with your head over a groupmate to preen, sit and Use Isleep after 5 minutes of rest during preferred time. shake in dusty/sandy areas to dust-bathe. Use Iclean after.
creatures.profileHtml:
🗺 Gondwa Habitats
Green ValleyGrand PlainsHunter’s ThicketTriad FallsTitan’s PassDarkwoodsWhistling ColumnsBig Quill LakeYoung GroveHoodoo Expanse.
🍖 Preferred Foods
InostranceviaDryosaurusStruthiomimusDilophosaurusAdolescentsDidelphodon (AI)
⏰ Active Time
Diurnal; Active in the day, sleeps during the night.
Use !sleep after 5 minutes of rest during preferred time.
🧼 Upkeep
Preening/Dust-bathing; shake with your head over a groupmate to preen, sit and shake in dusty/sandy areas to dust-bathe.
Use !clean after.
🪺 Nesting
Nesting must always happen in a habitat.
🍽 Food
Use !tasty in local chat when eating preferred food.
⚖️ Behaviors
Birds of a feather
Deinonychus can be seen grouping to massive numbers with Latenivenatrix due to their simularities. They may hunt with Latenivenatrix but Latenivenatrix take up 2 slots in the hunting group. Ex. If there is 10 slots, 5 Latenivenatrix take up all 10 slots. 3 Latenivenatrix would take up 6 slots. Those other 4 slots could be taken up by Deinonychus. Normal grouping is unlimited.
Nest Buddy
Deinonychus, during times of low population, may find themselves unable to find a suitable mate. During these times, they may mate, nest and group with a Latenivenatrix. The offspring will be hybrids of the two and may follow any Laten or Deino optional behaviors but themselves cannot nest offspring due to being infertile.
Mirror games
Deinonychus love reflections. They’ll gather near shiny surfaces or water puddles, watching themselves and performing mock dances or calls. This fascination sometimes extends to collecting metallic or glossy objects for their nests.
⚠️ May be considered a rulebreak
Deinonychus may mixpack with Latenivenatrix, even mating with them.
🧬 Basic Info
Can Symbiote As: Yes. T2+
Can Have Symbiote: No
Pest: Deinonychus cannot hunt any sauropods unless in a full group.
✍️ Elysian_Cedarcall📅 2025-04-12

rules.title: Hunting
rules.bodyHtml:
These are the hunting rules that uphold the realism of our fantasy semi-realism server. Here you will find how to hunt, when to hunt, and how the body down rules work. Hunting is allowed at 60% hunger or below by default. Doing so within Line of Sight (LOS) of another body down is not allowed unless it is glitched/blocked or an unclaimed Tier 1 body.
These rules make hunting not just about killing, but help make it a fun and tense experience with battles between life and death.

Who can hunt and when?

Hunting is allowed for any dinosaur capable of consuming meat when their hunger is at 60% or lower.

Hunting within LoS of another body is not allowed unless it is either an unclaimed Tier 1 body or glitched/inaccessible (e.g. deeply submerged in water, on a cliff where you can't get to it).

At least one pack member must be the correct hunger to initiate a hunt. That member doesn't need to start the hunt.

Once a body drops during a hunt, it belongs to the party that started the hunt, regardless of who died, to ensure fairness.

During a disaster, combat and hunting in shelter/caves is not allowed as long as you stay in that cave, being inside right before it hits and after it hits to heal.

  • 🌟Tip! To check your hunger and thirst, use the !info command; the bot will whisper you the stats in Global!

Hunt Timer and Failed Hunts

A hunt is considered failed when a target is not killed before the combat timer has ended, or a member of the hunting party dies. The target player or group can't be attacked for 30 minutes once the combat timer expires.

﻿If a hunt is successful, the 30min cooldown does not apply; it only applies to failed hunts.

Revenge Killing and Pack Hunting

Revenge killing or swapping to another dinosaur to harass a player/party in game is not allowed.

When you kill another player, you will receive a whisper telling you the name of the person you have killed.

When you get killed by a solo player, you will receive a whisper telling you the name of the player and their hunger percentage.

When killed by a pack, you will receive a whisper of the person that killed you, their hunger percentage, the name of the person with the lowest hunger in the group, along with their hunger.

Hunting Hunger

Keep in mind, if you get killed by a pack, another party member may have been the correct hunger, even if it shows an incorrect hunger of the one that had killed you, because only one group member needs to have 60% or less hunger to hunt.

rules.title: Group Sizes
rules.bodyHtml:
Group sizes define how many dinosaurs per tier are allowed to group up. This ensures that there aren't any megapacks of carnivores roaming around, providing better balancing and fair gameplay. Herbivores may have unlimited herd sizes but their defensive members limit is the designated group tier limit listed below.
Only sub-adult and adult dinos count towards these limits.

Tip! Remember that tag-teaming during combat is not allowed!

Max Group Sizes Per Tier:

Tier 1 → 10
Tier 2 → 8
Tier 3 → 4
Tier 4 → 3
Tier 5–6 → 2

rules.title: Mixpacking
rules.bodyHtml:
Mixpacking is the most common rule broken. It means helping or grouping with dinosaurs outside your species, which includes sharing food, letting other players scavenge with no consequences, helping ungrouped players quest, or protecting them, which are all prohibited unless your profile allows certain species to pack together.

Definition

- Helping an ungrouped dinosaur in any way counts as mixpacking.
- Grouping with a different species is not allowed, however there are some profiles that allow mixpacking behaviors. Make sure to read your dinosaur's profile.

Examples of Mixpacking

- Deinosuchus carrying a Struthiomimus across water.
- Hatzegopteryx flying a Pachy across Grand Plains.
- Helping waystone users outside your group.
- Assisting ungrouped dinos with hunts or quests (this includes giving free trophies).
- Helping in combat with buffs, nerfs, or body blocking when not grouped.

Exceptions

- Two dinosaur mods of the same species may group together, like the PT Rex and the Vanilla Rex.
- Not everything is considered mixpacking; there are symbiotic relationships where different tiered dinos are allowed to group together.

Punishment for Mix-/Overpacking

After the warning from staff is ignored and not corrected, face a 2h ban from in game.

Tip! Make sure to read the Symbiosis section for detailed explanations and information!

rules.title: Nesting
rules.bodyHtml:
Nesting in Elys-Astra is simple: any dinosaur, regardless of gender, may place a nest, incubate eggs, and have any gender as their partner. Nesting is a part of the more realistic aspect of Path, so there are a few rules to follow, but nothing close to what would be considered hardcore realism.

Who can nest?

- Any dinosaur can nest either solo or with any gendered partner; you don't need a partner of the opposite sex.
- Dinosaurs must be the same species to be allowed to nest together; the PT Rex and Vanilla Rex, for example.

What is allowed/not allowed during nesting?

- Nesting is only allowed in your dinosaur's preferred habitats, but can be placed anywhere within them.
- Only fully grown dinosaurs can nest. Players are not allowed to place down nests or get eggs when the parent is not an adult; this should not be possible anyways from in-game mechanics.
- To avoid overpopulation, players may only place and nest once per season with a max of 5 offspring unless that dinosaur's profile says otherwise.
- Nest-hopping is not allowed. You must stay for at least 30 minutes, unless both parents agree to let you go earlier. Players are not allowed to waste eggs by accepting nest/egg invites and then not joining.
- Spamming group, nesting, or egg invites is not allowed. Players that do this can be muted and egg invites from said players may be blocked for you.

How do I defend my nest?

- Destroying player nests is not allowed.
- As parents, players are allowed to defend within a 1 Spino length radius from their placed nest. They may not attack unprovoked and must aggro call at least 1 time to warn away intruders. This behavior follows the same guidelines for Personal Space.
- Parents may not chase intruders over 1 Spino length from the nest when they flee. They may defend until BD if the attacker does not leave after being warned.

rules.title: Roles
rules.bodyHtml:
In Elys-Astra, there are special roles with boosts to make you stronger. But each power has a price...
A curse is always slumbering within. Some of these roles give you power and some have a curse that is impossible to resist, implementing altered behavior. Some of them are so rare that they are limited...
Following a role's requirements is mandatory; breaking this will result in role removal and the loss of its power.
Limited Roles and Blessings cannot be traded or gifted. Any abuse will be punished with a complete inventory wipe.
Some Limited Roles are restricted to 1 of each type per group, with groups being defined as both main members and any symbiotes.

Alpha

Common - Unlimited
- Earned by migrating 24x with !migrationbuff or purchasing in SP Shop for 300 SP.
- Buffs:
- turn radius +0.15

Grouping Limitations
- Only 1 Alpha allowed per group.

Banshee

Common - Unlimited
- Earned through 5% random chance while playing on the server.

- Buffs:
- weight +375
- max stamina +50
- Debuffs:
- max health -100

Required Behaviors
- This role may not be equipped on aquatic dinosaurs due to the water factor.
- Must be brightly colored.
- Territorial towards other Banshees unless grouped (must share same diet to territory).
- Allowed to act friendly to lure prey closer by friendly calling and stalking.
- Avoids water; will not cross water or drink unless at 25% thirst or lower; rain intolerance is optional. If hunting target moves into water, hunt must stop unless requirement to be near water is met.

Optional Behaviors
- Broadcasts a lot every minute, running into trees and rocks. May bite teammates from time to time due to confusion and may attempt to bite at water before drinking from it.

Donator: @ErosDemi

Fallen

Common - Unlimited
- Earned through Trials and randomly upon either being nested during Bloodmoons or entering Sharptooth Marsh.
- :Buffs:
- speed +0.25
- turn radius +0.2
- Debuffs:
- weight -200

Required Behaviors
- Primary color must be pink or red.
- Can only hunt critters and dinos younger than Sub-Adult.
- Will court potential mates in Savannah Grasslands during Winter by the Ring of Fire.
- Courted dinos must be Fallen of the same species.

Optional Behaviors
- Can attack their own offspring if at 25% or less health while also actively starving (taking hunger damage). Babies that survive an attack must leave the group and will actively avoid their parents for a lifetime, remembering them.
- Enforces personal space towards anything Sub-Adult and younger.
- Fallen offspring will be hyper-aggressive towards their siblings, even chasing them off from their meals, but never to kill.

Donator: @𝓗𝓶𝓱

Matriarch

Common - Unlimited
- Earned by nesting 48 unique users or purchasing in SP Shop for 300 SP.

Grouping Limitations
- Only 1 Matriarch allowed per group.
- T1 & T2 → +2 additional packmates / defenders
- T3 to T6 → +1 additional packmate / defender

Repterus

Earned randomly by spawning.
- Buffs:
- weight +150
- speed +0.05
- Debuffs:
- armor -0.025

Roamer

Common - Unlimited
- Earned by completing 100 quests.
- Buffs:
- weight +400
- armor +0.1
- max stamina +60
- Debuffs:
- max health -150
- increased thirst drain

Grouping Limitations
- Can only group with other Roamers of the same habitat, tier, and diet. Symbiotes must be Tier 1 only.
Required Behaviors
- Primary color must be white or red.
- When thirst drops below 40%, must prioritize finding water over initiating hunts or contests (active combat may be finished).
- Cannot fully deplete any food source; must abandon bodies or vegetation at 50% food value or higher and move on.
- Must sleep within LOS of water; cannot use !sleep away from water sources.
- Cannot claim or initiate territory; must forfeit all territory disputes.
- Cannot hunt other Roamers.

Optional Behaviors
- Stand in rain or mist without seeking shelter.
- May kill AI critters and stand over them instead of eating.
- Shake head as if clearing confusion.
- Seek out and sit in mud patches.
- Stare at water sources before drinking.

Donator: @ForrNaught

Shepherd

Common - Unlimited
- Earned by migrating 24x with !migrationbuff or purchasing in SP Shop for 300 SP.
- Increases herd defense size by 2.5x max tier limit (ex: Eotriceratops from 2 → 5 defenders).

Grouping Limitations
- Herbivores only.
- Only 1 Shepherd allowed per group.

Albino & Melanistic

Uncommon - Unlimited
- Earned randomly by being nested.

Albino
- stamina regen +0.2
- turn radius +0.15
- max health -75
- armor -0.05

Melanistic
- knockback +0.3
- armor +0.05
- max stamina -75
- increased hunger drain

Ashen

Uncommon - Unlimited
- Earned by completing the Ring of Fire.
- Buffs:
- speed +0.1
- fall resistance +650
- max stamina +45
- carry capacity +500
- Acquiring this role unlocks the ability to use !sacrifice, which offers a 50% chance to kill your dino in exchange for any mutation, genetic, or role. This command has a 7 day cooldown.

Required Behaviors
- Ashen are not allowed to be colorful; must be gray/pale/dull/colorless.

Cursed

Uncommon - Unlimited
- Earned through 10% upon entering Burned Forest (1h cooldown).
- Buffs:
- weight +100
- max health +100
- turn radius +0.3

Required Behaviors
- Must aggressive call 3x upon entering a new POI.
- If a creature in your line of sight stays within 3 Spino-lengths of you for the time it takes you to aggressive call 2x, you must charge and attack them. Ignores body down.
- Once you reach 50% health, you must 4 call and retreat.

Donator: @kinich kinnie

Frostbitten

Uncommon - Unlimited
- Earned through chance after surviving a Whiteout event.
- Buffs:
- max health +300
- armor +0.1
- speed +0.1
- Debuffs:
- weight -400
- jump -0.6

Required Behaviors
- Constantly seeks warmth from warm POIs or the presence of other creatures.
- Frequently invades personal space in an attempt to warm up.
- Will rub against pack/herd mates and others regardless of danger.
- Must flee if attacked.
- May never fight back if personal spaced.
- During Autumn and Winter, this behavior becomes more persistent.
- During Autumn and Winter, may never initiate personal space against others.

Donator: @Zipley

Infested

Uncommon - Unlimited
- Earned by Infested event or drinking from infested water.
- Buffs:
- max health +175
- turn radius +0.15
- Debuffs:
- armor -0.05
- speed -0.05

Required Behaviors
- Must attack groupmates at 25% hunger (cannot avoid by leaving group).
Optional Behaviors
- Occasionally aggro call at rocks, trees, and water or jump of cliffs to show that your dinosaur's brain has been infested by parasitic bacteria!

Donator: @Annumara || MOD ||

Nomad

- Earned by visiting all land or water POI's. Start with /nomad in your first POI and then the bot will track your journey until the end. To check the current progress of your journey, use /nomadstatus.
- The role will be locked onto the dinosaur you have done this journey with. It cannot be swapped to another dinosaur.
- Buffs:
- speed +0.3
- fall resistance +300
- Debuffs:
- weight -500
- max health -50

Grouping Limitations
- Must be solitary; may not group up with anyone.
Required Behaviors
- Nomads must automatically flee every territory and contest and cannot accept them.
- Must be nomadic; they must keep wandering around.

Donator: @NovaChrome

Protector

Uncommon - Unlimited
- Earned by reaching max Elder on 3 different species.
- Buffs:
- max health +350
- health regen +0.3
- Allows players to defend young from unfair hunts.

Grouping Limitations
- Cannot group past 2; both Protectors have to be in the same tier.

Required Behaviors
- Only Tiers 2-4 may be Protectors.
- Must third-party only hunts between species of drastically different ages when in LOS.
- Hunts the predator(s) instead of simply distracting it if at 60% of lower hunger.
- Harasses creatures who attempt to hunt young by being a nuisance (e.g. running around them quickly, biting at their tail, etc.) until the young is out of LOS; harassment doesn't permit killing unless at appropriate hunger percentage.
- Defends itself from being hunted if it cannot escape.

Prohibited Behaviors
- Cannot hunt/eat any species below sub-adult at all.
- Cannot have offspring.
- Cannot be a Pest.
- Cannot enforce personal space.
- Cannot protect young from combat engagements outside of hunts (e.g. contests, territory, etc.)

Donator: @Elysian_Cedarcall

Repterus Mutatus (RMT)

Uncommon - Unlimited
- Earned through Miniboss event or Sharptooth (small chance, 1h cooldown).
- Buffs:
- weight +300
- speed +0.07
- Debuffs:
- max health -100
- armor -0.05
- increased hunger drain

Grouping Limitations
- Can only group with other RMT of the same habitat, tier, and diet. Symbiotes must be Repterus or RMT.

Required Behaviors
- Dino's colors must be green or blue only.
- Must prioritize hunting/contesting Ashen

Zephyrion

Uncommon - Limited
- Earned by surviving Whiteout or Windfang (5% chance).
- Buffs:
- weight +150
- speed +0.25
- turn radius +0.1

Grouping Limitations
- Only 2 allowed per group.

Required Behaviors
- Primary color must be purple.
- Only hunts T4 or lower. (Pests, critters, etc.) can scavenge.

Donator: @Ralosys

Apex, Splicer, & Vanguard

Rare - Limited
- Apex → Christmas → speed +0.25
- Splicer → Valentines → weight +575
- Vanguard → Halloween → armor +0.25

Grouping Limitations
- Only 1 of each role allowed per group.

Bounty Hunter (BH)

- Only obtainable through Trials and giveaways.
- Buffs:
- weight +350
- speed +0.15
- turn radius +0.2

Grouping Limitations
- Only allowed to group with other Bounty Hunters of the same species; max of 2.
Required Behaviors
- Will prioritize T4+.
- Will only hunt below T4 when hunger is below 20% (pests must still abide by pest rules).
- Will not back down/forfeit from a territory, spar, or hunt as they would rather die than lose their honor.

Optional Behaviors
- May hunt at 10% higher hunger than the active allowed percentage (ex. during Summer, may hunt at 70% instead of 60%).

Donator: @ChromeCam | Support

Cannibal

- Earned by being nested (events only) and Mystery Boxes.
- Buffs:
- combat weight +395
- speed +0.19
- turn radius +0.19

Grouping Limitations
- Cannot group or nest; is always solo.

Required Behaviors
- Carnivores: Can only leave their body if at 25% or less thirst (must return immediately once satiated) or a natural disaster/event occurs. Due to their extreme possessiveness, they will immediately contest, regardless of hunger percentage, if their body was claimed by someone else while they were away.
- Herbivores: May hunt at 60% hunger. Can only leave their body if at 25% or less thirst and/or hunger or a natural disaster occurs.
- When a cannibal is hatched in, it kills its siblings (important for parents to find cannibal young via !sniff and kill it as quickly as possible).
- Cannibals prefer to stalk before hunting; if spotted with an alert call while stalking, the cannibal stops and must commit to the hunt or leave the creature alone.
- If a cannibal herbivore is below 60% hunger and it kills another creature, it immediately gets +150 hunger points.

Dark Urge (DU)

Rare - Unlimited
- Earned through winning arena events or giveaways.
- Buffs:
- speed +0.1
- turn radius +0.25

Grouping Limitations
- Can only pack with other Dark Urges (can be different tiers but must be same habitat/diet).
- Only 3 max per group.

Required Behaviors
- Primary color must be red.
- Must enforce personal space.
- Only hunts T4+ or Pests and does not scavenge.
- Will not eat salt rocks or critters unless about to starve (10% hunger or less).
- Accepts any spar invite.

Donator: @𝒩𝓊𝓀𝒾𝒾 | @𝐿𝒾𝒶𝐵𝒾𝓉𝑒

Gluttony

Rare - Limited
- Earned by having every clamp-related dinosaur rebirthed at least once.
- Buffs:
- carry capacity +750
- armor +0.15
- max stamina +150
- hunger +60
- Debuffs:
- speed -0.3

Grouping Limitation
- Can only group with other Sin roles (NOT other Gluttons).
Required Behaviors
- Must only be applied to dinosaurs that have Clamp or Bonebreak.
- Must only hunt creatures it can clamp or bonebreak.
- Must be albino, melanistic, dark, or extremely light toned (black, white, grey, etc.).
- First attack must be a clamp or bonebreak (whether it hits or misses).
- Cannot tolerate other Gluttons and will attempt to purge them.
- Must always respect personal space.
- If a Glutton is discovered via !sniff, it must be hunted before any other hunt occurs.
- If discovered in a nest, the parent must kill and eat it.

Optional Behaviors
- Can hunt at 80% hunger or less at all times.
- Can scavenge.
- Can hunt siblings in nests at any time.
- Hunters may leave bodies once hunger is full.
- Hunts end when the hunter is completely out of stamina.

Donator: @MiracleHealer

Ice Breaker (IB)

Rare - Limited
- Earned through Christmas event mystery boxes.
- Buffs:
- weight +380
- max health +150
- armor +0.2

Grouping Limitations
- Only 1 allowed per group.

Kryptic Plague (KP)

Rare - Unlimited
- Earned only by meeting Kamona in-game and grouping.
- Buffs:
- weight +300
- speed +0.07

Required Behaviors
- Dino's primary color must be yellow or orange.
- Must prioritize hunting/contesting against Repterus Mutatus.

Donator: @Krypt

Mimic

Rare - Unlimited
- Earned through reaching max Elder on 10 different species.
- Buffs:
- max health +150
- max stamina +50
- armor +0.01
- Debuffs:
- weight -150

Grouping Limitations
- Can only infiltrate packs/herds of similar tier or higher creatures.

Required Behaviors
Must copy the coloration of the creature chosen to mimic.
Displays occasional behaviors of its true nature that may give away its mimicry.
At 25% food, must attack its "pack/herd" out of desperation.
Must stop attacking once the target reaches 50% health (DO NOT KILL).
May occasionally move away from its current pack/herd to feed according to its true diet.

Donator: @Horridon

Rabies

Rare - Unlimited
- Earned by grouping with Jessica or Chevy in-game.
- Buffs:
- weight +400
- speed +0.01
- turn radius +0.01
- Debuffs:
- max health -100

Grouping Limitations
- Only 2 max per group.

Required Behaviors
- This role may not be equipped on aquatic dinosaurs due to the water factor.
- Afraid of water; will refuse to drink unless about to dehydrate (10% thirst or less) and will try to "drown" itself in shallow water.
- Has outbursts of aggression towards its own species. May still group but will randomly attack and chase teammates until they snap out of it (will never kill them). Due to this, they prefer having symbiotes.
- Territories its own species on sight; does not wait until territory limit is met unless that group is bigger than 2 of the same species. May choose to forfeit after some time in combat.

Donator: @Creature

Ravenous

Rare - Limited
- Earned by killing 100 fully grown dinos at 25% or less hunger.
- Buffs:
- speed +0.25
- acceleration speed +0.25
- weight +250
- Debuffs:
- max stamina -50
- hunger drain +0.45

Grouping Limitations
- Each player with Ravenous in the group decreases maximum group or defender size by 1.
- Unlimited amount of Ravenous per group up to meeting the reduced grouping/defender limit.

Required Behaviors
- ﻿Must aggressive call 3x and defend bodies from their own groupmates for up to 30 seconds, only backing down once it has eaten its fill or is wounded (less than 50% health).
- Will become irritable towards its groupmates when below hunting hunger, aggro-calling and pacing, enforcing personal space, and often contesting for nearby food sources like critters, bones, or berry bushes.
- Once a Ravenous host nears starvation (20% or less), it will begin a hunt on its own symbiote. If a Ravenous symbiote nears starvation (20% or less), it must either hunt its host or abandon the group for a minimum of 30 minutes. Only other Ravenous creatures may assist in hunting symbiotes or hosts.

Donator: @WhyYaMakeMeMad, Bane of Babies

Sentinel

Rare - Unlimited
- Earned by grouping with Kamona in-game and must also possess Alpha or Vanguard.
- Buffs:
- turn radius +0.25
- armor +0.2
- knockback +0.1

Grouping Limitations
- May only pack with other Sentinels.
OR
- May only form a pack of 2 with a chosen mate of any tier within the same diet; no symbiotes.

Solo Sentinel Behaviors
- Will not claim territory.
- May choose a lone herbivore to claim, following it around and using it as bait for other predators, intercepting them once they approach.
- The chosen herbivore can defend the Sentinel but must disengage at 50% health or less.
Sentinel Group Behaviors
- Grouping limit follows the highest tier in the group.
- May only hunt at 40% or less hunger.
- Must stay in a fixed POI for at least 1 hour; may only leave earlier if their hunger or thirst drops to 25% or less.
- Cannot forfeit territory challenges.

Bonded Behaviors
- The chosen mate becomes the Sentinel's territory.
- Sentinel has to remain within LOS of their mate.
- Sentinels are extremely possessive; will immediately attack anything within their mate's personal space and will only back off once the target leaves it.
- If their mate initiates the contact, the Sentinel cannot attack.

Donator: Anonymous

rules.title: Scavenging
rules.bodyHtml:
Some small dinosaurs cannot hunt large prey all on their own, especially when afraid of those larger than them. To ensure that they can survive, these so called scavengers can steal bites of meat or berries from a food resource-guarded by an intimidating owner.
Scavenging always comes with a risk...
Are you willing to take it?

Tip! Trophies can be stolen through scavenging.

What is Scavenging?

Scavenging is a "grab and go" method of stealing food from an already claimed resource. This does not mean that scavengers can just walk up to a body and eat from it; they have to run up, take a chunk of meat or berries (maybe a trophy if the thief is feeling brave), and run away with them.
Only Tier 1 and Tier 2 can scavenge.
You must be hunting hunger to scavenge.
When in a group, if one member is at 60% hunger and another is at 70%, the one at 70% cannot participate in scavenging.
If you can't eat it, you can't scavenge it (trophies excluded).

Scavenging Combat

The body owner can always defend their body, but they can't chase scavengers over 1 Spino length away.
Scavenging is a risk - scavengers attacked by body owners may not fight back.
If a scavenger is killed while scavenging, other scavengers may continue but risk being killed as well.
Combat Timer does not apply during scavenging. If a group member dies, the body owner may keep defending, while scavengers may still scavenge.

Grab & Go

Tiers 1-2 may scavenge but must immediately run away with the food.
Once their hunger reaches 60% or more while stealing, scavengers must stop.

rules.title: Seasonal Rules & Behaviour
rules.bodyHtml:
Survival in Elys-Astra follows the rhythm of the seasons. Each season changes how dinosaurs act, migrate, guard, and survive. Players must roleplay seasonal changes realistically. If a specific behavior is not listed, you may follow any from your species profile that apply.

Spring

Season of renewal and growth.
As vegetation breaks through the remnants of winter, creatures turn their focus on recovering from the jaws of the cold by reclaiming previous resources and territory. Securing a mate and producing a bloodline take the highest priority. Males turn vibrant with color as they search for mates to form families with.
─ · ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ ─ · ─ · ─
Rutting & Dominance
Rivalries intensify in fertile POI's; displays, intimidation, sparring, and territory contests are common.
Males only spar other brightly colored males for mates; females may compete or select.
Sparring does not end in death (follow Sparring rules).

Summer

Season of abundance and thirst.
Everyone begins to feel the claws of the searing heat; competition falls away to self-perseverance. Animals huddle around waterholes and under the canopy of trees, hoping to quench their thirst and fend off the scorch, while predators save their remaining energy to stalk exhausted and resting prey.
─ · ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ ─ · ─ · ─
Keeping Cool
The heat quickly drains stamina, so rest under shade or relax in or near water sources to keep from overheating.
Water Guarding
Resource guarding a water source is forbidden in the Migration Zone.
Groups of 3+ may guard small ponds that have a water percentage of 25% or less in an effort to save it for themselves.
Solo Tier 4-6 semi-aquatics may guard large lakes that have a water percentage of 25% or less.
Tiers 1–3 cannot guard water regardless of water value.

Autumn

Season of preparation and apprehension.
As the heat retracts its claws, another threat looms in the gradually dropping temperatures; the time to tighten down the hatches is now. Carnivores are testy, using their impatience to swiftly fill ravenous bellies. Those that faced previous hardships struggle to fully ready themselves for what's around the corner.
─ · ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ ─ · ─ · ─
Desperate Measures
Carnivores must stay with their bodies until the food value drops to 50% or lower, thirst drops to 25% or lower, the body despawns, or they get contested off of it.
Hunting
Allowed at 80% hunger or less.

Winter

Season of scarcity and endurance.
The harsh cold sinks its fangs into every creature, blanketing the world in a frigid stillness. Every step, every breath, costs them energy they can't afford. Tense carnivores capitalize on weakened prey and rivals to quell their starvation as herbivores tirelessly protect any delicacies they can uncover from the frost.
─ · ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ · ─ ─ · ─ · ─
Food Guarding & Dominance
Herbivores may guard !tasty foods if rivals are present.
Dominant groupmates eat first and may defend food.
Disputes resolved by intimidation displays or sparring, never in death.
Hunting
Allowed at 40% hunger or less.

rules.title: Sparring & Personal Space
rules.bodyHtml:
Sparring

Sparring is optional.
To spar, shake or broadcast call while looking at a player. If they do the same, the spar may start. If they don’t, then they didn’t accept it.
Sparring never ends in death; you are not allowed to kill the player. To signal that you are low, crouch and move your head away.

Personal Space

Personal space is optional to enforce.
1 Spino length around you is considered your personal space. If another dinosaur enters it, you may aggressive call at them once. If they don’t leave after 5 seconds, you may attack.
If you are getting attacked for personal space, you may not fight back and must distance yourself to 3+ Spino lengths away.
Those enforcing personal space may not chase further than 3 Spino lengths.

rules.title: Symbiosis
rules.bodyHtml:
Symbiosis is a method of establishing a mutually beneficial relationship between two unlikely species. Tiers 1-2 may symbiote for higher tiers and must share the same diet and habitat. Dinosaurs that have 2 habitat such as flier x semi aquatic may symbiote for flier only. Symbiotes can scout locations, locate resources, and track hunts while the hosts offer easy meals and protection. When the host nests, the symbiote can assist with building the nest. Symbiotes do not nest when they are in symbiosis, but can help protect their host's young.
Symbiotes do not count towards a group's overall size limit, but do count when Limited Roles with 1 type per group limitations are equipped.

How does symbiosis work?

Tiers 2-3 may have 2 Symbiotes.
Tiers 4-6 may have 1 Symbiotes.
To recruit a symbiote, the protector/host simply invites them. These symbiotes are considered unbonded.
Unbonded symbiotes cannot help in fights/hunts/territory and cannot use buff/nerf calls.
To obtain a bonded symbiote, the host must approach a suitable symbiote with food to officially bond with them. This can happen after grouping up.
Bonded symbiotes may assist fully in all combat except territory.

rules.title: Natural Disasters & Events
rules.bodyHtml:
The creatures that roam the lands of Elys-Astra are not the only dangerous things you have to worry about.
Extreme weather events and natural disasters are Mother Nature's way of enforcing the survival of the fittest; heavy droughts, extreme storms, and even terrifying meteor showers are part of her arsenal.
Other unexplainable phenomenon, like the ominous Blood Moon and deadly Windfang disease, make navigating Elys-Astra even more tricky.
Taking precautions when the signs of imminent disasters rear their heads could truly be the difference between life and death.
How will you survive the elements?

Important: Combat during disasters that require shelter is not allowed in shelter/caves. This applies the second the first warning drops and users start going into shelter to hide.

Thunderstorm

- Spring
- Disaster that causes disadvantageous side effects the longer you stay out in the torrential downpour; 3 total waves of stat debuffs.
- Each wave causes loss of armor (-0.15), max stamina (-30), max health (-250), movement speed (-0.1), and combat weight (-500)
- Being inside a Home Cave or designated shelter will protect you from the harsh weather conditions.
- Survival grants armor (+0.05), max stamina (+10), max health (+75), movement speed (+0.05), combat weight (+125), and growth (+0.1)

Heatwave

- Summer
- Disaster that causes quickened thirst depletion due to droughts caused by increased temperatures.
- Staying near available fresh water and resting in shade/mud can increase your chances of survival.
- Survival grants health regen (+1)

Windfang

- Autumn
- Epidemic event that causes the entire population to catch a disease which causes induced hunger loss; 3 total waves of vomiting.
- Each wave causes immediate loss of hunger, dropping it to 0%.
- Being around an abundant source of food or eating just enough to stop hunger damage can increase your chances of survival.
- Survival grants stamina regen (+1)

Whiteout

- Winter
- Disaster that causes disadvantageous side effects the longer you stay out in the cold; 3 total waves of stat debuffs.
- Each wave causes loss of armor (-0.15), max stamina (-30), max health (-250), movement speed (-0.1), and combat weight (-500)
- Being inside a Home Cave or designated shelter will protect you from the harsh weather conditions.
- Survival grants armor (+0.05), max stamina (+10), max health (+75), movement speed (+0.05), combat weight (+125), and growth (+0.1)

Meteor Strike

- Any season
- Galactic event where multiple meteors smash into the planet and cause mass annihilation and temporary wildfires.
- Causes immediate death to those who are not sheltered and health reduction to those that are sheltered when the meteors hit.
- Survival grants armor (+0.05), max stamina (+10), max health (+75), movement speed (+0.05), combat weight (+125), and growth (+0.1). Also offers a random chance to obtain 500 Stardust.

Blood Moon

- Any season
- Lunar event that causes hyper-aggression in herbivores exposed to moonlight.
- Herbivores that are not sheltered when the Blood Moon is exposed must aggro all nearby creatures, even group members.
- No buffs upon survival.

Infestation

- Any season
- Epidemic event that causes a random portion of the population to become Infected (gives the Infested role).
- No buffs upon survival.

Solar Eclipse

- Any season
- Lunar event that provides a rare blessing in the form of an Eclipse Mutation (Triple Trait Mutation).
- Each player has a 25% chance to obtain the mutation during the event.

Miniboss

- Any season
- Combat event that mutates one random player into a Miniboss for a short period of time.
- Provides increased stat and causes hyper-aggression.
- Stat buffs include combat weight (+2000) and turn radius (?)
- Miniboss events follow normal combat rules with the exception of hunger percentage and BD being ignorable and third-partying being allowed.
- Minibosses are enraged; they attack anything within reach, even group members, and will not run away from combat. They do not fear death.
- Those that survive as the Miniboss get to keep half of their stat buffs (until death, logging, or server restart) and obtain the Repterus Mutatus role.
- Miniboss-Killer keeps some of the stats.

rules.title: Territory & Migration Zones
rules.bodyHtml:
Territory is enforced when a dinosaur wants / needs to claim a POI. Any dinosaur may claim territory, but apexes are especially aggressive and naturally driven to dominate. Territory disputes may only occur within the same tier, diet and habitat. It is always mandatory for carnivores and optional for herbivores when the max group size limit per POI is reached. For example, if a POI has 2 Gigas and 1 Acro, only two T5's are allowed, so they must fight until there are only two T5's left, or until one party forfeit. It is allowed below max but becomes mandatory at max limit per POI.

Initiating Territory

Can not hunt the target when territory is required, max limit per POI is met. Territory must be done over hunting. May not turn into a hunt.
Initiate from 2-Spino lengths of your opponent. If they can hit you with a non-AoE attack without moving, you are too close. Once at the appropriate distance, aggro call 3 times.
Opponents must respond with an aggro call to accept. If they don't, it counts as an automatic forfeit and they must leave the POI. They cannot return for 30 minutes or risk getting KOS'd by the party that challenged them.
Combat continues until the limit per POI is reached or one party forfeits.
You may forfeit a territory even if you initially accept the challenge.
Territory ignores body down until the maximum population of the tier is restored.
Territory is optional for carnivores while the POI is still within its population limit, but mandatory once the POI max is exceeded. Territory is completely optional for herbivores. Once the limit is reached, territories, not hunts, must be initiated.

Forfeiting Territory

To forfeit, turn away and flee while giving repeated alert calls.
If you walk or fly away without returning to combat within 10 seconds, it will count as auto forfeit and you must leave or risk getting KOS'd as long as they stay in the POI they got challenged away from.
The challenger must let the forfeiting dinosaur leave and cannot continue attacking them. They may, however, follow them to make sure that they leave the POI. If they don’t leave and keep roaming in it, they may continue to be attacked.

rules.title: Your Behaviour
rules.bodyHtml:
We aim for a respectful but relaxed environment. Casual conversation, disagreement, and jokes are allowed.. harassment, slurs, or targeted hostility are not.
Repeated false accusations against staff after clarification may result in moderation action.
Legitimate concerns should be reported through proper channels.
Elys Staff is held to a very high standard. No misconduct from both sides will be tolerated.

Behavioural Violations

No disrespectful behaviour towards anyone.
No spreading misinformation. (if gameplay related - gameplay behaviour infraction)
No advertising servers or other things in any shape or form.
No name-calling /insults
No disruptive arguments or disputes
No spam
No location dropping others, location dropping yourself is fine
Public discussions of rulebreaks are allowed if it is to positively remind if a rulebreak has happened. Must stop discussion if being told by staff.
Do not “mini-mod” or publicly accuse players of rulebreaking.
Metagaming
Loopholing
Targeting
No DMing staff members. All things are handled within tickets.

  • 🌟This is a PGe13 server. Any stuff that is not breaking server rules or involved with this server such as private dating issues is not for us to handle. Resolve private issues privately as long as all involved parties are at legal age of 18+.
  • 🌟Punishment: 1-hour mute and for sever cases, ban.

NSFW Content

No NSFW jokes, content, or language in chat, profiles, or roleplay.
No slurs in usernames, bios, jokes.
No “kys,” threats, or sexist/biased remarks.

  • 🌟Punishment: Permanent ban without appeal.

System Abuse

No abusing roles, commands, or inventory. Violations will lead to a complete inventory wipe including roles / the role that was misused gets removed.

Examples: stacking mutations, using !migrationbuff outside zones, restricted cmds (!tasty, !sleep, !clean) w/out profile permission.

Rulebreaks

Infraction 1 → Warning
Infraction 2 → Warning
Infraction 3 → Ban with appeal
Infraction 4 → Ban without appeal
Death by rulebreak: 1 full-growth item

  • 🌟Only thing that is not appealable are perma mute words (unless proven typo /context matters) and severe behaviour violations.

rules.title: Genetics & Mutations
rules.bodyHtml:
Smaller buffs that are very helpful for survival, slightly modifying your dinosaur's stats by the stat it's written for.
Genetics are common, found throughout your journey in this server.

How to get Genetics

You can get genetics simply by being nested in, or via /shop where they can be purchased with Survival Points (SP), which you gain automatically and passively just by playing!

Types of Genetics

Speed - Faster movement speed
Turn - Improved turn radius
Buff / Nerf Calls - Longer call duration
Jump - Slightly increase your jump height
Survival - For faster bleed/venom heal
Oxygen - Increase the time you can stay underwater

How to apply

To apply a genetic, use /assign and select your dinosaur species. You can have 1 mutation and 1 of each genetic type equipped to a dino at a time.

rules.title: Migrations
rules.bodyHtml:
As seasons change and resources ebb and flow, all creatures heed the instinctual call to follow the wave of Mother Nature's abundance. Herbivores are coaxed by flourishing vegetation and reliable nesting spots, while carnivores capitalize on this migratory behavior for consistent meals. With the changing seasons and passing time, dinos begin to show their age, growing alongside their experiences, struggles, and victories, before eventually falling into place in the circle of life.

What is migration & how does it work?

Migration is the act of following specific locations in order across the map to mimic the natural passing of seasons and resources.
Migration Zones, or MZ's, are selected POIs that make up these specific locations in the migratory cycles.
MZ's offer more resources than other POIs, including prominent nesting locations, increased food sources, and cleaner water.
Land Migration Cycles are followed by land, flier, and semi-aquatic dinos.
Ocean Migration Cycles are followed by semi and full-aquatic dinos.
Migration is mandatory for herbivores in a herd of 3 or more.

Gondwa Land Migrations

Cycle One
Spring - Triad Falls
Summer - Titan's Pass
Autumn - Birchwoods
Winter - Desolate Pass

Cycle Two
Spring - Hunter's Grove/Snake Valley
Summer - Burned Forest
Autumn - Sand Caverns/Big Quill Lake
Winter - Stego Mountain

Cycle Three
Spring - Green Valley
Summer - Whistling Columns
Autumn - Grand Plains
Winter - White Cliffs

Gondwa Ocean Migrations

Cycle One
Spring - Seagrass Bay
Summer - Golden Kelp
Autumn - Red Kelp Forest
Winter - The Teeth

Cycle Two
Spring - Volcano Bay
Summer - Sunken Hoodoos
Autumn - Sweetgrass Shallows
Winter - Golden Plateau

Cycle Three
Spring - Red Reef
Summer - Ocean Stacks
Autumn - Kelp Forest
Winter - Sweetwater Shallows

Migration Zone Behavior

The max population for each tier is doubled in migration zones (e.g. Tiers 5-6 doubles from 2 to 4, Tier 4 doubles from 3 to 6, etc.).
Territory rules still apply in MZ's; carnivores must territory each other when the max capacity of each tier is exceeded.
When a party forfeits from a territory in an MZ, they must stay out of LOS of the party that challenged them.
Due to some MZ's being smaller than others, the forfeiting party has one chance to stay out of LOS after initially forfeiting. If they re-enter LOS a second time, they can be KOS'd by the challenging party.

rules.title: Ring of Fire
rules.bodyHtml:
A ring of fire will exist in Savanna Grassland, if you enter this ring of fire and survive long enough.
Only those who survive their flesh burning in the fire, may be rewarded with The Ashen role that is gifted by Zorak, the Ancient Guardian of Fire and Chaos.
Entering this ring is at your own risk. Survive or die.

The Ashen

+0.1 MovementSpeedMultiplier
+650 FalldeathSpeed (less fall damage)
+45 MaxStamina
+500 CarryCapacity

Unlock access to a command called !sacrifice for 50% chance to kill your dinosaur and receive one of ANY mutation, genetic, or role that Zorak will steal for you. This has a cooldown of 7 days (only works with role equipped).
Ashen are not allowed to be colorful and must be gray/pale/dull/colorless.
Will be targeted by Repterus Mutatus users.

rules.title: Blessings & Trials
rules.bodyHtml:
Trials are places of the gods Rhaegorian and Tolouya
who open their doors for worthy players to try and
get the chance of getting blessed. Be worthy of
your pure heart or get punished by being reborn
as a baby for your greed.

Trials

Trials are places of the gods Rhaegorian and Tolouya
who open their doors for worthy players to try and
get the chance of getting blessed. Be worthy of
your pure heart or get punished by being reborn
as a baby for your greed.

Rhaegorian's Trial

This shrine is located in Sharptooth Marsh.

Tolouya's Trial

This shrine is located in Snake Gully.

Rewards

Getting blessed by the shrine can give you the strongest powers in the game. Those contain one of the following per use:

Mythos Blessing
Everflow Blessing
Titan Mutation
Triple Trait Mutation
Limited Role

How to obtain

This is an event like any other which can happen randomly but it is rare. When the announcement pops up, you have 15 minutes to get to one of the trials and prove your loyalty to the gods. Use !bless to try your luck inside the shrines. This command can only be used once per try every time the event activates.
Titan Mutation and Blessings are not allowed to be gifted or traded.

How this works

There is a 50/50 chance that you either win one of the rewards or lose for being greedy.

Combat Rules

Combat such as hunting, territory or personal space in those POI is not allowed but only if the event is actively running.
